scaffolds.description: Anchor program crate manifest with anchor-lang, anchor-spl, sha2 dependencies
Program entry point with declare_id!, module declarations, and #[program] instruction handlers
State module barrel export for all account structs
OperatorConfig PDA account struct (singleton, authority/treasury/mint/fee/paused)
CustomerBalance PDA account struct (per-customer balance, caps, counters)
AgentPackage PDA account struct (package metadata, split_bps, counters)
TaskReceipt PDA account struct and TaskStatus enum
Instructions module barrel export for all instruction handlers
initialize_operator instruction: creates singleton OperatorConfig PDA
create_customer_account instruction: creates CustomerBalance PDA for signer
register_agent_package instruction: permissionless AgentPackage PDA creation
update_agent_package instruction: author-only field updates
deposit instruction: SPL token transfer from customer ATA to vault PDA
withdraw instruction: SPL token transfer from vault PDA to customer ATA
update_spending_caps instruction: customer-only cap updates
pause instruction: operator-only circuit breaker toggle on
unpause instruction: operator-only circuit breaker toggle off
Utility module with hash_seed SHA-256 helper and PDA seed constants
Anchor error_code enum with all program error variants
Anchor Mocha smoke tests: 6 core integration tests
Anchor workspace configuration with program ID and cluster settings

decisions.summary: Anchor v0.30+ workspace initialization with SHA-256 PDA seed derivation
decisions.reasoning: The program uses SHA-256 hashes of string identifiers (package_id, task_id) as PDA seeds rather than raw strings. This avoids the 32-byte PDA seed length limit for arbitrary-length identifiers while maintaining deterministic derivation across on-chain and off-chain contexts.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Raw string seeds (limited to 32 bytes), Keccak256 (non-standard for Solana ecosystem)

decisions.summary: Mint-agnostic OperatorConfig stores mint Pubkey rather than hardcoding USDC
decisions.reasoning: Storing the mint Pubkey in OperatorConfig allows the program to work with any SPL token (USDC, USDT, or test tokens) without code changes. The vault PDA is derived from the mint key, ensuring token account isolation.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Hardcoded USDC mint address, multiple mint support per operator

decisions.summary: Vault PDA as program-owned token account with seeds [b"vault", mint.key()]
decisions.reasoning: A single vault PDA per mint simplifies accounting and avoids per-customer token accounts. The program signs transfers out of the vault using PDA authority seeds, which is the standard Anchor pattern for custodial token accounts.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Per-customer vault accounts, escrow per task

decisions.summary: Daily spending cap uses calendar day (daily_reset_day as u64) not slot-based windows
decisions.reasoning: Calendar-day resets are more intuitive for customers and operators. The daily_reset_day field stores the day number (unix_timestamp / 86400), rolling over when the current day exceeds the stored value. This aligns with D9 decision from the specification.

decisions.summary: Author ATA validated but NOT created during register_agent_package
decisions.reasoning: Per D11, the author's associated token account for the configured mint is passed as an account and validated to exist, but the program does not create it. This keeps the instruction simpler and avoids requiring the associated-token-program account in the instruction. Authors must create their ATA before registering a package.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Program creates ATA via init_if_needed, deferred ATA validation at settlement time

interfaces.name: OperatorConfig
interfaces.description: Singleton PDA account holding program-wide operator settings. Seeds: [b"operator_config"]. Created once via initialize_operator, mutable only by the authority for pause/unpause. Stores the accepted token mint, treasury destination, and protocol fee in basis points.

members.name: authority
members.description: Operator wallet authorized for admin operations (pause, unpause, settle)

members.name: treasury
members.description: Destination wallet for protocol fee and non-agent-split revenue

members.name: mint
members.description: SPL token mint accepted by this program instance (e.g., USDC)

members.name: paused
members.description: Circuit breaker flag; when true, settlement instructions are blocked

interfaces.name: CustomerBalance
interfaces.description: Per-customer PDA tracking deposited balance, spending history, and configurable spending caps. Seeds: [b"customer_balance", customer.key().as_ref()]. One per customer wallet. Daily cap resets on calendar day boundary.

members.name: customer
members.description: Customer wallet public key (also used in PDA seed)

members.name: balance
members.description: Current available balance in token base units

members.name: task_count
members.description: Number of tasks settled against this customer

members.name: max_per_task
members.description: Maximum charge allowed per individual task

members.name: max_per_day
members.description: Maximum aggregate charge allowed per calendar day

members.name: daily_spent
members.description: Amount spent in the current calendar day

members.name: daily_reset_day
members.description: Day number (unix_timestamp / 86400) of last daily reset

members.name: created_at
members.description: Account creation timestamp from Clock sysvar

interfaces.name: AgentPackage
interfaces.description: Marketplace listing PDA for an agent package. Seeds: [b"agent_package", sha256(package_id)]. Permissionless creation; signer becomes author. Tracks revenue attribution and quality metrics.

members.name: author
members.description: Package author wallet; receives split_bps share on quality settlements

members.name: split_bps
members.description: Author's revenue share in basis points (0-10000)

members.name: total_earned
members.description: Lifetime earnings credited to this package's author

members.name: task_count
members.description: Total tasks processed using this package

members.name: active
members.description: Whether the package is available for new tasks

interfaces.name: TaskReceipt
interfaces.description: Immutable settlement receipt PDA created once per task. Seeds: [b"task_receipt", sha256(task_id)]. PDA init constraint prevents duplicate task IDs. Records the full settlement outcome for auditability.

members.name: task_id
members.description: Unique task identifier matching the off-chain system

members.name: amount
members.description: Total amount charged in token base units (0 if quality not met)

members.name: author_earned
members.description: Amount paid to the agent package author

members.name: quality_met
members.description: Whether the task output met quality attestation criteria

members.name: agent_package
members.description: Associated agent package PDA, if any

members.name: receipt_hash
members.description: SHA-256 hash of the off-chain receipt JSON for integrity verification

members.name: status
members.description: Current status: Settled, Refunded, or Disputed

interfaces.name: TaskStatus
interfaces.description: Enum representing the lifecycle state of a task settlement receipt.

members.name: Settled
members.description: Task has been settled and charges applied

members.name: Refunded
members.description: Task settlement was reversed; customer credited back

members.name: Disputed
members.description: Task is under dispute (reserved for future use)

interfaces.name: BillingError
interfaces.description: Anchor error_code enum covering all validation failures across the program.

members.name: InsufficientBalance
members.description: Customer balance insufficient for this operation

members.name: ExceedsDailyCap
members.description: Amount would exceed daily spending cap

members.name: Unauthorized
members.description: Signer is not authorized for this operation

members.name: InvalidMint
members.description: Token mint does not match configured mint

members.name: InvalidAmount
members.description: Amount must be greater than zero

interfaces.name: hash_seed
interfaces.description: SHA-256 hash helper for deterministic PDA seed derivation. Produces a 32-byte digest from an arbitrary-length string input. Used by all string-keyed PDAs (AgentPackage, TaskReceipt) and must match the off-chain TypeScript/Rust implementations in Tasks 3 and 5.

interfaces.name: PDA Seed Constants
interfaces.description: Canonical seed byte strings used for all PDA derivations, exported from utils/mod.rs for consistency across instructions.

interfaces.name: initialize_operator
interfaces.description: Creates the singleton OperatorConfig PDA. Must be called once before any other instruction. The signer becomes the authority. Stores the treasury pubkey, accepted token mint, and protocol fee rate.

members.name: treasury
members.description: Treasury wallet to receive protocol fees and non-split revenue

interfaces.name: create_customer_account
interfaces.description: Creates a CustomerBalance PDA for the signing customer wallet. Initializes all counters to zero and sets spending caps from arguments.

members.name: max_per_day
members.description: Maximum allowed aggregate charge per calendar day

members.name: customer
members.description: Customer wallet; becomes customer_balance.customer and pays rent

interfaces.name: register_agent_package
interfaces.description: Permissionless instruction creating an AgentPackage PDA. The signer becomes the author. Validates split_bps <= 10000 and that the author's ATA for the configured mint exists (but does not create it).

members.name: package_id
members.description: Unique package identifier; SHA-256 hashed for PDA seed

members.name: split_bps
members.description: Author's revenue share in basis points

members.name: operator_config
members.description: Read to get configured mint for ATA validation

members.name: author_ata
members.description: Author's existing ATA for the configured mint; validated not created

interfaces.name: update_agent_package
interfaces.description: Author-only instruction to update mutable fields on an existing AgentPackage. All arguments are optional; only provided values are applied.

members.name: split_bps
members.description: New split basis points if provided; must be <= 10000

interfaces.name: deposit
interfaces.description: Transfers SPL tokens from the customer's ATA to the program vault PDA. Increments balance and total_deposited counters on CustomerBalance.

members.name: amount
members.description: Amount to deposit in token base units; must be > 0

interfaces.name: withdraw
interfaces.description: Transfers SPL tokens from the program vault PDA back to the customer's ATA. Validates amount <= balance and decrements the balance counter. Uses PDA signer seeds for the vault authority.

members.name: amount
members.description: Amount to withdraw in token base units; must be > 0 and <= balance

interfaces.name: update_spending_caps
interfaces.description: Customer-only instruction to update max_per_task and max_per_day on their CustomerBalance PDA.

interfaces.name: pause
interfaces.description: Operator-only instruction that sets operator_config.paused = true, blocking all settlement instructions.

interfaces.name: unpause
interfaces.description: Operator-only instruction that sets operator_config.paused = false, re-enabling settlement instructions.

notes: This is the foundational task producing the on-chain program and IDL that all downstream tasks depend on. Task 2 adds settle_task and refund_task instructions to this same program. Task 3 writes comprehensive integration tests against this program. Task 5 builds the off-chain sidecar that submits transactions to these instructions — its PDA derivation and hash_seed implementation must exactly match the on-chain logic. The vault PDA initialization approach (whether in initialize_operator or a separate instruction) should be clearly documented as Task 2 will also interact with the vault during settlement. The generated IDL at target/idl/cto_billing.json and TypeScript types at target/types/cto_billing.ts are critical build artifacts consumed by Tasks 3, 5, and other downstream work.

programs/cto-billing/src/instructions/se.purpose: Implements the settle_task instruction handler with three-case quality-gated payment splitting (no agent/quality met/quality not met), validation chain (pause, auth, caps, balance), PDA-signed vault transfers, and TaskSettled event emission.
decision_notes: Optional agent_package and author_ata accounts handled via Anchor's Option<Account<>> (v0.30+) or two separate instruction variants (settle_task_default vs settle_task_with_package) — decision impacts IDL shape and client complexity
Daily cap rollover computed as current_day = clock.unix_timestamp / 86400; resets daily_spent if day boundary crossed
Case 3 (quality_met=false) charges zero to customer but still creates TaskReceipt and increments agent task_count
Vault PDA signer seeds pattern: &[b"vault", mint.key().as_ref(), &[vault_bump]] used for all CPI transfers
programs/cto-billing/src/instructions/se.context: Core billing engine instruction — the program's primary value proposition. Three settlement cases: (1) no agent → full to treasury, (2) agent + quality_met → split via split_bps, (3) agent + !quality_met → zero charge. TaskReceipt PDA init with hash_seed(&task_id) provides protocol-level idempotency (duplicate task_id fails at account creation).

programs/cto-billing/src/instructions/re.purpose: Implements the refund_task instruction that reverses a settled task by crediting customer balance and transferring funds from treasury back to vault, with hackathon simplification where 5D Labs absorbs author payment losses.
decision_notes: HACKATHON SIMPLIFICATION: Refund comes from treasury — if author was already paid, 5D Labs absorbs the loss; post-hackathon should implement clawback or delayed settlement window
Validates task_receipt.status == TaskStatus::Settled before allowing refund
Treasury owner (operator) must sign the transfer from treasury_ata back to vault
programs/cto-billing/src/instructions/re.context: Reversal mechanism for settled tasks. Operator-authorized only. Changes TaskReceipt status to Refunded and emits TaskRefunded event. Treasury funds the refund regardless of prior author payouts.

programs/cto-billing/src/events.rs.purpose: Defines all Anchor #[event] structs for off-chain indexing and observability: TaskSettled, TaskRefunded, Deposited, Withdrawn, AgentPackageRegistered.
decision_notes: Events appear in generated IDL for client-side parsing
TaskSettled includes Optional<Pubkey> for agent_package to distinguish Case 1 from Cases 2/3
programs/cto-billing/src/events.rs.context: Event definitions emitted via emit!() macro in instruction handlers. Used by settlement sidecar (Task 5) and off-chain indexers to track billing activity without polling accounts.

programs/cto-billing/src/instructions/mo.purpose: Barrel export module that re-exports all instruction handler modules including settle_task and refund_task alongside Task 1 instructions.
programs/cto-billing/src/instructions/mo.context: Modified from Task 1 to add pub mod settle_task and pub mod refund_task exports.

programs/cto-billing/src/lib.rs.purpose: Program entry point with declare_id!, module declarations, and #[program] block registering all instruction handlers including settle_task and refund_task.
decision_notes: settle_task and refund_task registered as public instruction handlers in the #[program] module
Events module imported so emit!() calls in instructions resolve correctly
programs/cto-billing/src/lib.rs.context: Modified from Task 1 scaffold to register the two new settlement instructions. The settle_task function signature accepts task_id, amount, receipt_hash, quality_met. The refund_task function takes no additional args beyond accounts.

tests/settlement.ts.purpose: 8-case integration test suite covering all three settlement cases, refund flow, spending cap enforcement, pause blocking, and duplicate task_id rejection on local validator.
decision_notes: Tests verify on-chain account state after each instruction, not just transaction success
Error cases verify specific Anchor error codes (ExceedsPerTaskCap, ExceedsDailyCap, ProgramPaused)
Duplicate task_id test relies on PDA init constraint violation
All 8 tests must pass within 30 seconds on local validator
Test setup reuses Task 1's initialization (operator config, customer account, agent package, deposits)
tests/settlement.ts.context: Comprehensive settlement test suite validating the core billing engine. Tests: (1) no agent → treasury, (2) agent + quality → split, (3) agent + !quality → zero charge, (4) refund restores balance, (5) per-task cap, (6) daily cap, (7) pause blocks settlement, (8) duplicate task_id fails.

programs/cto-billing/src/state/task_rece.purpose: TaskReceipt PDA account struct storing immutable settlement records and TaskStatus enum (Settled/Refunded/Disputed), with PDA seeds using SHA-256 hash of task_id for idempotent settlement.
decision_notes: PDA seeds: [b"task_receipt", hash_seed(&task_id)] — SHA-256 hash ensures deterministic derivation from arbitrary string task IDs
TaskStatus enum with Settled, Refunded, Disputed variants
Receipt is init'd during settle_task and mutated only during refund_task (status change)
programs/cto-billing/src/state/task_rece.context: Defined in Task 1 scaffold, actively used by Task 2's settle_task (init) and refund_task (status mutation). The init constraint on settle_task prevents duplicate task_id settlement at the protocol level.

programs/cto-billing/src/state/customer_.purpose: CustomerBalance PDA account struct tracking per-customer balance, spending caps (max_per_task, max_per_day), daily spending with day rollover, and cumulative counters.
decision_notes: Daily cap rollover: daily_reset_day stores the day number (unix_timestamp / 86400); if current_day > daily_reset_day, daily_spent resets to 0
balance field debited during settlement, credited during refund
task_count incremented on every settlement regardless of case
programs/cto-billing/src/state/customer_.context: Defined in Task 1, critical to Task 2's validation chain and settlement accounting. The daily cap logic with day boundary rollover is implemented in settle_task's validation.

programs/cto-billing/src/state/agent_pac.purpose: AgentPackage PDA account struct storing marketplace package metadata including split_bps for revenue sharing, activity counters (total_earned, task_count, success_count), and active status.
decision_notes: split_bps determines author's share: author_amount = (amount * split_bps) / 10000
task_count incremented in both Case 2 and Case 3; success_count only incremented in Case 2 (quality_met=true)
active field validated in Case 2 — inactive packages cause PackageInactive error
programs/cto-billing/src/state/agent_pac.context: Defined in Task 1, mutated by Task 2's settle_task to track earnings and success metrics per agent package.

programs/cto-billing/src/errors.rs.purpose: Anchor error_code enum with all program error variants used across settlement validation: InsufficientBalance, ExceedsPerTaskCap, ExceedsDailyCap, Unauthorized, ProgramPaused, InvalidSplitBps, DuplicateTaskId, InvalidMint, PackageInactive, InvalidAmount.
decision_notes: PackageInactive error used when agent_package.active == false in Case 2
ProgramPaused checked first in validation chain for both settle_task and refund_task
programs/cto-billing/src/errors.rs.context: Defined in Task 1, referenced by Task 2 instructions for validation error reporting. Tests verify specific error codes are returned.

programs/cto-billing/src/utils/mod.rs.purpose: Utility module with SHA-256 hash_seed helper for deterministic PDA seed derivation and PDA seed constants (OPERATOR_CONFIG_SEED, CUSTOMER_BALANCE_SEED, AGENT_PACKAGE_SEED, TASK_RECEIPT_SEED, VAULT_SEED).
decision_notes: hash_seed used for TaskReceipt PDA derivation: seeds = [TASK_RECEIPT_SEED, &hash_seed(&task_id)]
Same hash_seed function used by AgentPackage PDA derivation
Must match client-side hashSeed implementation in tests/helpers.ts
programs/cto-billing/src/utils/mod.rs.context: Defined in Task 1, actively used by Task 2 for TaskReceipt PDA seed computation.

programs/cto-billing/src/state/operator_.purpose: OperatorConfig singleton PDA account storing authority pubkey, treasury pubkey, accepted mint, protocol_fee_bps, and paused circuit breaker flag.
decision_notes: authority field checked against operator signer in both settle_task and refund_task
paused field checked as first validation in settlement chain
programs/cto-billing/src/state/operator_.context: Defined in Task 1, read by Task 2 instructions for authorization and pause checks.

title: Comprehensive Anchor Program Integration Test Suite — Overview
content:
## Purpose
Task 3 builds a thorough Mocha/TypeScript integration test suite for the `cto-billing` Anchor program, exercising every instruction, error path, and edge case on localnet. It is the quality gate that CI (Task 9) will run.

## Architecture
- **`tests/helpers.ts`** — shared utilities: mock mint creation, ATA helpers, PDA derivation (SHA-256 matching on-chain `hash_seed`), SOL airdrop.
- **`tests/cto-billing.ts`** — 12 `describe` groups, 30+ `it` cases, covering all 11 instructions and all error codes.
- **`package.json`** — `test:ci` script (`anchor test -- --timeout 60000`).

## Dependency Chain
Task 3 depends on **Task 1** (core instructions) and **Task 2** (settle_task, refund_task, events). The test suite validates both tasks' implementations end-to-end.

## File Map
| File | Action | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `tests/helpers.ts` | create | PDA derivation, token helpers, airdrop |
| `tests/cto-billing.ts` | create | Full 12-group test suite |
| `package.json` | modify | Add `test:ci` script |

## Key Decisions Pending
1. **Circuit-breaker scope** — Should `deposit`/`withdraw` be blocked when paused, or only `settle_task`/`refund_task`? Group 9 tests assume only settlement/refund are blocked per PRD language.
2. **Daily-cap rollover testing** — Local validator may not support clock manipulation; tests may only verify within a single day boundary.

title: Test Helper Utilities — Interface & Implementation Guide
Test Groups 1–3: Initialization, Customer Accounts, Agent Packages
content:
## Subtask 3002 — 10 Test Cases

### Group 1: Initialization (2 tests)

**Test 1 — `initializes operator config with correct fields`**
- Call `initialize_operator(treasury, protocol_fee_bps=0)` with operator as signer.
- Accounts: `operator_config` PDA (init), `mint` (mock USDC), `system_program`.
- Assertions: `authority == operator.publicKey`, `treasury` matches, `mint` matches, `protocol_fee_bps == 0`, `paused == false`.

**Test 2 — `fails on re-initialization`**
- Call `initialize_operator` again with identical seeds.
- Assert: Anchor error (PDA already in use / account already initialized).
- Pattern: `try { await program.methods.initializeOperator(…); assert.fail() } catch (err) { /* verify error */ }`.

### Group 2: Customer Account Management (3 tests)

**Test 3 — `creates customer account with caps`**
- Call `create_customer_account(max_per_task=50_000_000, max_per_day=200_000_000)`.
- Seeds: `[b"customer_balance", customer.publicKey.toBytes()]`.
- Assertions: `balance == 0`, `total_deposited == 0`, `total_spent == 0`, `task_count == 0`, caps match.

**Test 4 — `updates spending caps`**
- Call `update_spending_caps(max_per_task=75_000_000, max_per_day=300_000_000)`.
- Assertions: both caps updated; other fields unchanged.

**Test 5 — `fails creating duplicate customer account`**
- Call `create_customer_account` again for same customer → PDA collision error.

### Group 3: Agent Package Registration (5 tests)

**Test 6 — `registers a package`**
- Call `register_agent_package(package_id="test-agent-v1", split_bps=3000, source_uri="https://github.com/test/agent")`.
- PDA seed: `[b"agent_package", SHA256("test-agent-v1")]`.
- Assertions: `author == author.publicKey`, `split_bps == 3000`, `active == true`, `total_earned == 0`, `task_count == 0`, `success_count == 0`.

**Test 7 — `updates package split_bps`**
- Call `update_agent_package(split_bps=Some(2500))`.
- Assert: `split_bps == 2500`.

**Test 8 — `deactivates package`**
- Call `update_agent_package(active=Some(false))`.
- Assert: `active == false`.

**Test 9 — `fails update by non-author`**
- Sign with `secondAuthor` → `BillingError::Unauthorized`.

**Test 10 — `fails register with split_bps=10001`**
- Call `register_agent_package` with `split_bps=10001` → `BillingError::InvalidSplitBps`.

### Error Assertion Pattern
```typescript
try {
  await program.methods.registerAgentPackage("bad", 10001, "uri")
    .accounts({...}).signers([author]).rpc();
  assert.fail("Should have thrown");
} catch (err) {
  expect(err.error.errorCode.code).to.equal("InvalidSplitBps");
}
```

title: Test Groups 4–5: Deposits, Withdrawals, and Settlement Case 1
content:
## Subtask 3003 — 7 Test Cases

### Group 4: Deposits & Withdrawals (5 tests)

**Setup**: Ensure customer account exists. Create customer ATA, mint 200 USDC to it. Derive vault PDA.

**Test 1 — `deposits 100 USDC`**
- Call `deposit(amount=100_000_000)`.
- Accounts: `customer` (signer), `customer_balance` PDA (mut), `customer_ata` (mut), `vault` PDA (mut), `operator_config`, `mint`, `token_program`.
- Assertions: `customer_balance.balance == 100_000_000`, `customer_balance.total_deposited == 100_000_000`. Vault token account balance increased by 100_000_000.

**Test 2 — `deposits another 50 USDC`**
- Call `deposit(amount=50_000_000)`.
- Assertions: `balance == 150_000_000`, `total_deposited == 150_000_000`.

**Test 3 — `withdraws 30 USDC`**
- Call `withdraw(amount=30_000_000)`.
- Assertions: `balance == 120_000_000`. Customer ATA received 30 USDC back.

**Test 4 — `fails withdraw exceeding balance`**
- Call `withdraw(amount=200_000_000)`.
- Assert: `BillingError::InsufficientBalance`.

**Test 5 — `withdraws remaining balance to zero`**
- Call `withdraw(amount=120_000_000)`.
- Assert: `balance == 0`.

### Group 5: Settlement — Case 1, No Agent Package (2 tests)

**Setup**: Deposit 100 USDC. Record treasury ATA balance before settlement.

**Test 6 — `settles TASK-001 for 15 USDC — full to treasury`**
- Call `settle_task(task_id="TASK-001", amount=15_000_000, receipt_hash=[..32 bytes..], quality_met=true)`.
- No `agent_package` or `author_ata` passed (or `None`).
- Assertions:
  - `customer_balance.balance == 85_000_000`.
  - Treasury ATA balance increased by 15_000_000.
  - Fetch `TaskReceipt` PDA via `deriveReceiptPda("TASK-001")`:
    - `task_id == "TASK-001"`, `amount == 15_000_000`, `customer` matches, `author_earned == 0`, `quality_met == true`, `status == Settled`, `agent_package == None`.

**Test 7 — `verifies customer counters`**
- `customer_balance.task_count == 1`, `customer_balance.total_spent == 15_000_000`.

### Token Balance Verification Pattern
```typescript
const vaultInfo = await getAccount(provider.connection, vaultPda);
assert.equal(vaultInfo.amount.toString(), "100000000");
```
Use `getAccount` from `@solana/spl-token` and compare `.amount` (bigint) as strings for precision.

title: Test Groups 6–8: Agent Splits, Quality-Not-Met, Spending Caps
content:
## Subtask 3004 — 8 Test Cases

### Group 6: Settlement — Case 2, Quality Met with Split (3 tests)

**Setup**: Customer has balance ≥ 20 USDC. Agent package `test-agent-v1` is active with `split_bps=3000`. Record author ATA and treasury ATA balances.

**Test 1 — `settles TASK-002 with 30% author split`**
- Call `settle_task(task_id="TASK-002", amount=20_000_000, receipt_hash=[..], quality_met=true)` with `agent_package` and `author_ata` accounts.
- Split math: `author_earned = 20_000_000 * 3000 / 10000 = 6_000_000`. Treasury receives `14_000_000`.
- Assertions:
  - Customer balance decreased by exactly 20_000_000.
  - Author ATA increased by 6_000_000.
  - Treasury ATA increased by 14_000_000.

**Test 2 — `verifies AgentPackage counters`**
- `agent_package.total_earned == 6_000_000`, `task_count == 1`, `success_count == 1`.

**Test 3 — `verifies TaskReceipt fields`**
- `amount == 20_000_000`, `author_earned == 6_000_000`, `quality_met == true`, `agent_package == Some(packagePda)`, `status == Settled`.

### Group 7: Settlement — Case 3, Quality Not Met (2 tests)

**Test 4 — `settles TASK-003 with quality_met=false — zero charge`**
- Call `settle_task(task_id="TASK-003", amount=20_000_000, quality_met=false)` with agent_package.
- Assertions: Customer balance **unchanged**. Author ATA **unchanged**. Treasury ATA **unchanged**.
- TaskReceipt: `amount == 0`, `author_earned == 0`, `quality_met == false`.

**Test 5 — `verifies AgentPackage counters after quality-not-met`**
- `task_count` incremented (now 2). `success_count` unchanged (still 1). `total_earned` unchanged.

### Group 8: Spending Cap Enforcement (3 tests)

**Setup**: Create `secondCustomer` with known caps. Deposit sufficient funds.

**Test 6 — `enforces per-task cap — ExceedsPerTaskCap`**
- Set `max_per_task=10_000_000` via `update_spending_caps`. Attempt `settle_task` for 15_000_000.
- Assert: `BillingError::ExceedsPerTaskCap`.

**Test 7 — `enforces daily cap — ExceedsDailyCap`**
- Set `max_per_day=25_000_000`. Settle successfully for 20_000_000. Then attempt 10_000_000.
- Assert: `BillingError::ExceedsDailyCap` (20 + 10 = 30 > 25).

**Test 8 — `tests day boundary rollover logic`**
- If clock warp is available (`bankrun` or validator CLI `warp-slot`), advance by 86400 seconds, verify `daily_spent` resets.
- If not feasible: document limitation, verify day-number logic in a unit-style assertion (e.g., confirm `daily_reset_day` was set on first settlement and `daily_spent` accumulated correctly within the current day).

### Split Calculation Detail
```
author_earned = (amount * split_bps) / 10_000
treasury_receives = amount - author_earned
```
All arithmetic is in u64 lamports. Integer division — no rounding issues at these amounts.

title: Test Groups 9–12: Circuit Breaker, Refunds, Idempotency, Authorization
content:
## Subtask 3005 — 11 Test Cases

### Group 9: Circuit Breaker (5 tests)

**Test 1 — `pauses the program`**
- Call `pause()` with operator signer.
- Fetch `OperatorConfig` PDA → `paused == true`.

**Test 2 — `blocks settlement when paused`**
- Attempt `settle_task` → `BillingError::ProgramPaused`.

**Test 3 — `allows deposit when paused`**
- Call `deposit(amount)` → succeeds.
- Rationale: PRD specifies only settlement/refund are blocked. This is a **decision point** — if the on-chain program also blocks deposits, update this test to expect `ProgramPaused` and add a comment.

**Test 4 — `unpauses the program`**
- Call `unpause()` with operator → `paused == false`.

**Test 5 — `allows settlement after unpause`**
- Call `settle_task` → succeeds.

### Group 10: Refund Flow (2 tests)

**Setup**: Deposit funds, settle `TASK-REFUND-001`.

**Test 6 — `refunds a settled task — balance restored`**
- Record customer balance before refund.
- Call `refund_task` for `TASK-REFUND-001`.
- Assertions:
  - Customer balance restored by the original settlement amount.
  - `TaskReceipt.status == Refunded`.
  - Treasury ATA balance reduced (treasury absorbs author payout if any — hackathon simplification).

**Test 7 — `fails refund on already-refunded task`**
- Call `refund_task` again for `TASK-REFUND-001`.
- Assert: error (constraint `task_receipt.status == TaskStatus::Settled` fails → `BillingError::Unauthorized` or Anchor constraint error).

### Group 11: Idempotency (1 test)

**Test 8 — `fails settling duplicate task_id`**
- Attempt `settle_task` with `task_id` already used (e.g., `TASK-001`).
- Assert: Anchor PDA init error (account already in use). The `init` constraint on `task_receipt` PDA prevents duplicates.

### Group 12: Authorization (3 tests)

**Test 9 — `blocks settle_task by non-operator`**
- Sign `settle_task` with `customer` keypair instead of `operator`.
- Assert: `BillingError::Unauthorized` (constraint `operator == operator_config.authority`).

**Test 10 — `blocks pause by non-operator`**
- Sign `pause()` with `customer` → `BillingError::Unauthorized`.

**Test 11 — `blocks unpause by non-operator`**
- Sign `unpause()` with `author` → `BillingError::Unauthorized`.

### Test Ordering Strategy
Mocha runs tests sequentially within a `describe` block. The suite is designed so Groups 1-12 can run in order, building on shared on-chain state. Each group's `before` hook ensures prerequisites are met.

### Full Suite Summary
| Group | Tests | Key Instructions Covered |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 2 | initialize_operator |
| 2 | 3 | create_customer_account, update_spending_caps |
| 3 | 5 | register_agent_package, update_agent_package |
| 4 | 5 | deposit, withdraw |
| 5 | 2 | settle_task (no agent) |
| 6 | 3 | settle_task (quality met, split) |
| 7 | 2 | settle_task (quality not met) |
| 8 | 3 | settle_task (cap enforcement) |
| 9 | 5 | pause, unpause, settle_task |
| 10 | 2 | refund_task |
| 11 | 1 | settle_task (idempotency) |
| 12 | 3 | settle_task, pause, unpause (auth) |
| **Total** | **36** | **All 11 instructions, all error codes** |

## Critical Alignment Points

2. SHA-256 hash function (`hashSeed` in TS) must produce identical output to on-chain `hash_seed` in Rust.
