Include all dependencies: @coral-xyz/anchor, @solana/web3.js, @solana/spl-token, chalk (v5), ora, commander. Include dev dependencies: typescript, tsx, @types/node. Add scripts: demo:localnet runs 'tsx src/index.ts --cluster localnet', demo:devnet runs 'tsx src/index.ts --cluster devnet', build runs 'tsc', typecheck runs 'tsc --noEmit'.

description: TypeScript configuration for the demo CLI project
instructions: Create tsconfig.json with target ES2022, module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext, strict true, outDir dist, rootDir src, esModuleInterop true, resolveJsonModule true, skipLibCheck true, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames true. Include src/**/*.ts in the include array.

description: PDA derivation helpers matching on-chain SHA-256 seed logic
instructions:
Implement PDA derivation functions that exactly match the on-chain program's seed logic. Import PublicKey from @solana/web3.js and createHash from crypto.

Export a hashSeed function that takes a string input and returns a Buffer by computing SHA-256: `createHash('sha256').update(input).digest()`.

Export these functions, each returning { pda: PublicKey, bump: number }:

1. deriveOperatorConfig(programId: PublicKey) — seeds: [Buffer.from('operator_config')]. Use PublicKey.findProgramAddressSync.

2. deriveCustomerBalance(programId: PublicKey, customer: PublicKey) — seeds: [Buffer.from('customer_balance'), customer.toBuffer()].

3. deriveAgentPackage(programId: PublicKey, packageId: string) — seeds: [Buffer.from('agent_package'), hashSeed(packageId)]. This is critical: the on-chain program uses SHA-256 hash of the package_id string as the seed, not the raw string.

4. deriveTaskReceipt(programId: PublicKey, taskId: string) — seeds: [Buffer.from('task_receipt'), hashSeed(taskId)]. Same SHA-256 hashing as agent package.

5. deriveVault(programId: PublicKey, mint: PublicKey) — seeds: [Buffer.from('vault'), mint.toBuffer()].

All seed constants must exactly match those in programs/cto-billing/src/utils/mod.rs: OPERATOR_CONFIG_SEED = b'operator_config', CUSTOMER_BALANCE_SEED = b'customer_balance', AGENT_PACKAGE_SEED = b'agent_package', TASK_RECEIPT_SEED = b'task_receipt', VAULT_SEED = b'vault'.

description: Formatting utilities for Explorer links, USDC formatting, and phase headers
instructions:
Import chalk from 'chalk' (v5 ESM default import). Export these functions:

1. explorerLink(signature: string, cluster: string): string — returns `https://explorer.solana.com/tx/${signature}?cluster=${cluster}`. For 'localnet', use cluster='custom&customUrl=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8899'.

2. accountExplorerLink(address: string, cluster: string): string — returns `https://explorer.solana.com/address/${address}?cluster=${cluster}` with same localnet handling.

3. formatUsdc(lamports: number): string — divides by 1_000_000 and returns a string with exactly 2 decimal places using .toFixed(2).

4. phaseHeader(num: number, title: string): void — logs a blank line, then a chalk.bold.cyan line like '═══ Phase {num}: {title} ═══'.

5. logSuccess(msg: string): void — logs chalk.green('  ✓ ' + msg).

6. logInfo(msg: string): void — logs chalk.gray('  → ' + msg).

7. logExplorer(label: string, signature: string, cluster: string): void — logs chalk.dim('  🔗 ' + label + ': ' + explorerLink(signature, cluster)).

description: Mock receipt JSON generation and SHA-256 hashing for the receipt_hash parameter
instructions:
Import createHash from 'crypto'. Export an interface ReceiptJson with fields: task_id (string), customer (string), duration_seconds (number), infra_tier (string), provider (string), agent_used (string), itemized_costs (array of {description: string, amount_usdc: number}), total_usdc (number), timestamp (string).

Export function buildMockReceipt(taskId: string, customerPubkey: string, agentUsed: string, totalUsdc: number): ReceiptJson — returns a receipt with:
- task_id: taskId
- customer: customerPubkey
- duration_seconds: 120
- infra_tier: 'standard'
- provider: '5D Labs'
- agent_used: agentUsed
- itemized_costs: [{description: 'Compute (2 min)', amount_usdc: totalUsdc * 0.6}, {description: 'Storage & I/O', amount_usdc: totalUsdc * 0.3}, {description: 'Network egress', amount_usdc: totalUsdc * 0.1}]
- total_usdc: totalUsdc
- timestamp: new Date().toISOString()

Export function computeReceiptHash(receipt: ReceiptJson): Uint8Array — serializes the receipt to JSON using JSON.stringify (with keys sorted for determinism — use a replacer or sort the object), then computes SHA-256 and returns the digest as a Uint8Array (32 bytes). This hash must be passed as the receipt_hash parameter to settle_task on-chain.

description: CLI argument parsing with commander and Anchor program/provider setup
instructions:
Import { Command } from 'commander', { Connection, Keypair } from '@solana/web3.js', { AnchorProvider, Program, Wallet } from '@coral-xyz/anchor', and fs.

Export interface CliOptions { cluster: 'localnet' | 'devnet'; keypairPath: string; }.

Export function parseArgs(): CliOptions — use commander to define a program with --cluster option (choices: ['localnet', 'devnet'], default 'localnet') and --keypair option (default '~/.config/solana/id.json'). Parse and return.

Export function getRpcUrl(cluster: string): string — returns 'http://127.0.0.1:8899' for localnet, 'https://api.devnet.solana.com' for devnet.

Export function createProvider(connection: Connection, payer: Keypair): AnchorProvider — creates a wallet from the keypair using the Wallet class, constructs an AnchorProvider with opts { commitment: 'confirmed', preflightCommitment: 'confirmed' }.

Export function loadProgram(provider: AnchorProvider): Program — imports the IDL from '../../target/idl/cto_billing.json' (using createRequire or a JSON import with assert), creates and returns a new Program instance. Use the program ID from the IDL.

Handle the case where the IDL file doesn't exist with a clear error message directing the user to run 'anchor build' first.

description: Phase 0: Setup & Token Provisioning — keypair generation, airdrop, mint, ATAs, token minting
instructions:
Import Keypair, Connection from @solana/web3.js, createMint/getOrCreateAssociatedTokenAccount/mintTo from @solana/spl-token, and formatting utilities.

Export interface DemoState containing: operator (Keypair), customer (Keypair), agentAuthor (Keypair), mint (PublicKey), customerAta (PublicKey), authorAta (PublicKey), treasuryAta (PublicKey), cluster (string), connection (Connection), program (Program).

Export async function phase0(connection: Connection, program: Program, cluster: string): Promise<DemoState>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(0, 'Setup & Token Provisioning').
2. Generate 3 keypairs: operator, customer, agentAuthor using Keypair.generate().
3. If cluster is 'localnet': airdrop 10 SOL to each keypair. Use connection.requestAirdrop with 10 * LAMPORTS_PER_SOL. After each airdrop, call connection.confirmTransaction with the signature. Log each airdrop.
4. Create mock USDC mint: call createMint(connection, operator, operator.publicKey, null, 6). The operator is both payer and mint authority. Log the mint address.
5. Create ATAs: call getOrCreateAssociatedTokenAccount for customer (owner=customer.publicKey), agentAuthor (owner=agentAuthor.publicKey), and treasury (owner=operator.publicKey). Payer is operator for all. Log each ATA address.
6. Mint 1000 USDC to customer ATA: call mintTo(connection, operator, mint, customerAta.address, operator, 1_000_000_000). Log the mint amount.
7. Log all pubkeys with logInfo, and Explorer links for the mint using accountExplorerLink.
8. Return the DemoState object with all generated data.

Wrap the entire function body in a try/catch. On error, log the error message with chalk.red and re-throw. Use ora spinner for long operations (airdrop, mint creation).

description: Phase 1: Initialize Operator — creates the singleton OperatorConfig PDA
instructions:
Import DemoState, PDA helpers, formatting utils, and Anchor types.

Export async function phase1(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(1, 'Initialize Operator').
2. Derive the OperatorConfig PDA using deriveOperatorConfig(state.program.programId).
3. Call state.program.methods.initializeOperator(state.treasuryAta, 0).accounts({ authority: state.operator.publicKey, operatorConfig: operatorConfigPda, mint: state.mint, systemProgram: SystemProgram.programId }).signers([state.operator]).rpc().
4. The protocol_fee_bps is 0 as specified.
5. After the transaction, fetch the OperatorConfig account: state.program.account.operatorConfig.fetch(operatorConfigPda).
6. Log: OperatorConfig PDA address, authority, treasury, mint, protocol_fee_bps, paused status.
7. Log the transaction signature and Explorer link.

Note: The Anchor accounts struct for InitializeOperator requires: authority (signer, mut), operatorConfig (init PDA), mint (Account<Mint>), systemProgram. Ensure the accounts object matches the on-chain struct field names exactly (check the IDL — Anchor auto-converts to camelCase).

description: Phase 2: Register Agent Package — author registers with 30% split
instructions:
Export async function phase2(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(2, 'Register Agent Package').
2. Define packageId = 'smart-debug-agent-v1', splitBps = 3000, sourceUri = 'https://github.com/example/smart-debug-agent'.
3. Derive AgentPackage PDA using deriveAgentPackage(state.program.programId, packageId).
4. Derive OperatorConfig PDA for the accounts.
5. Call state.program.methods.registerAgentPackage(packageId, splitBps, sourceUri).accounts({ author: state.agentAuthor.publicKey, agentPackage: agentPackagePda, operatorConfig: operatorConfigPda, authorAta: state.authorAta, systemProgram: SystemProgram.programId }).signers([state.agentAuthor]).rpc().
6. Fetch the AgentPackage account and verify fields.
7. Log: AgentPackage PDA, author wallet (state.agentAuthor.publicKey), '30% revenue split', source_uri, active status.
8. Log transaction signature and Explorer link.

Note: The RegisterAgentPackage accounts struct includes operatorConfig and authorAta in addition to author and agentPackage. Ensure these are all provided. The author_ata must be the agentAuthor's ATA for the configured mint.

description: Phase 3: Create Customer Account — customer creates balance account with spending caps
instructions:
Export async function phase3(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(3, 'Create Customer Account').
2. Define maxPerTask = 100_000_000 (100 USDC), maxPerDay = 500_000_000 (500 USDC).
3. Derive CustomerBalance PDA using deriveCustomerBalance(state.program.programId, state.customer.publicKey).
4. Call state.program.methods.createCustomerAccount(new BN(maxPerTask), new BN(maxPerDay)).accounts({ customer: state.customer.publicKey, customerBalance: customerBalancePda, systemProgram: SystemProgram.programId }).signers([state.customer]).rpc().
5. Use BN from @coral-xyz/anchor (import { BN } from '@coral-xyz/anchor' or 'bn.js') for the u64 arguments.
6. Fetch CustomerBalance account and verify fields.
7. Log: CustomerBalance PDA, max_per_task as formatted USDC ('100.00 USDC'), max_per_day as formatted USDC ('500.00 USDC'), balance (should be 0).
8. Log transaction signature and Explorer link.

description: Phase 4: Customer Deposits USDC — 200 USDC from customer ATA to vault
instructions:
Export async function phase4(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(4, 'Customer Deposits USDC').
2. Define amount = 200_000_000 (200 USDC).
3. Derive CustomerBalance PDA and Vault PDA using helpers.
4. Derive OperatorConfig PDA.
5. Call state.program.methods.deposit(new BN(amount)).accounts({ customer: state.customer.publicKey, customerBalance: customerBalancePda, customerAta: state.customerAta, vault: vaultPda, operatorConfig: operatorConfigPda, mint: state.mint, tokenProgram: TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID }).signers([state.customer]).rpc().
6. Import TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID from @solana/spl-token.
7. After confirmation, fetch CustomerBalance to get the new balance.
8. Log: deposit amount ('200.00 USDC'), new balance (formatUsdc), Explorer link.

IMPORTANT: The vault PDA is a token account. If the vault doesn't exist yet (first deposit), the program may need to create it. Check if the on-chain deposit instruction initializes the vault or if it must pre-exist. Based on the Deposit accounts struct, the vault has seeds = [VAULT_SEED, mint.key().as_ref()] and is marked mut but NOT init — so the vault token account must already exist. You may need to create it as part of Phase 1 (initialize_operator) or as a separate step. If the program doesn't handle vault creation, add a vault initialization step here using createAccount + initializeAccount or via a separate program instruction. Check the on-chain code carefully. If there's no explicit vault initialization instruction, the vault PDA may need to be created as a token account with the PDA as owner before the first deposit. Use the @solana/spl-token createAccount or a raw transaction to create this.

description: Phase 5: Settle Task — Quality Met with 30/70 author/treasury split
instructions:
Export async function phase5(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(5, 'Settle Task — Quality Met (Author Gets Paid)').
2. Define taskId = 'CTO-1234', amount = 10_000_000 (10 USDC), qualityMet = true.
3. Build mock receipt using buildMockReceipt(taskId, state.customer.publicKey.toBase58(), 'smart-debug-agent-v1', 10.0).
4. Compute receiptHash using computeReceiptHash(receipt) — returns Uint8Array of 32 bytes. Convert to Array<number> if Anchor expects number[] for [u8; 32].
5. Derive all required PDAs: OperatorConfig, CustomerBalance (for the customer), TaskReceipt (for taskId), Vault, AgentPackage (for 'smart-debug-agent-v1').
6. Derive or compute vault_authority PDA if needed (the SettleTask struct has a vault_authority UncheckedAccount).
7. Build the settle_task call: state.program.methods.settleTask(taskId, new BN(amount), Array.from(receiptHash), qualityMet).accounts({ operator: state.operator.publicKey, operatorConfig: operatorConfigPda, customerBalance: customerBalancePda, treasuryAta: state.treasuryAta, vault: vaultPda, vaultAuthority: vaultAuthorityPda, taskReceipt: taskReceiptPda, mint: state.mint, tokenProgram: TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID, systemProgram: SystemProgram.programId, clock: SYSVAR_CLOCK_PUBKEY, agentPackage: agentPackagePda, authorAta: state.authorAta }).signers([state.operator]).rpc().
8. Fetch TaskReceipt PDA to get actual amount, author_earned, quality_met.
9. Log with chalk.green.bold: '🟢 Quality MET — Author earned 3.00 USDC (30%), Treasury received 7.00 USDC (70%)'.
10. Log: task_id, amount_charged, author_earned, TaskReceipt PDA, Explorer link.

Note on vault_authority: This is the PDA that has signing authority over the vault token account. It's typically derived with seeds like [b'vault'] or [b'vault_authority'] or the vault PDA itself acts as authority. Check the on-chain SettleTask struct for the vault_authority seeds. If it's an UncheckedAccount with no explicit seeds constraint, it may be derived as [b'vault', mint.key().as_ref()] with the program ID, or there may be a separate authority seed. Look at how the vault token transfers are signed in the on-chain settle_task implementation. For now, derive it as findProgramAddressSync([Buffer.from('vault'), mint.toBuffer()], programId) if it shares the vault PDA pattern, or as a separate [Buffer.from('vault_authority')] seed.

description: Phase 6: Settle Task — Quality NOT Met (Customer Protected)
instructions:
Export async function phase6(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(6, 'Settle Task — Quality NOT Met (Customer Protected)').
2. Define taskId = 'CTO-1235', amount = 15_000_000 (15 USDC), qualityMet = false.
3. Compute a receipt hash (can reuse buildMockReceipt with this task's data).
4. Derive all required PDAs same as phase5 but with taskId='CTO-1235'.
5. Fetch CustomerBalance BEFORE the settle to record the pre-settlement balance.
6. Call settle_task with quality_met=false, including the agent_package PDA.
7. Fetch CustomerBalance AFTER to verify balance is unchanged.
8. Fetch TaskReceipt PDA — verify amount=0 (or the original amount but with zero actual charge based on the on-chain Case 3 logic), quality_met=false.
9. Log with chalk.red.bold: '🔴 Quality NOT MET — Customer not charged. Author earned nothing.'
10. Log: task_id, amount_charged=0, author_earned=0, customer balance unchanged.
11. Log Explorer link.

Note: Per the on-chain settlement logic Case 3 (quality_met=false with agent package), the customer is NOT charged and no token transfers occur. The TaskReceipt is still created with amount=0. The AgentPackage.task_count is incremented but success_count is NOT.

description: Phase 7: Settle Task — Default Agent (No Split, full to treasury)
instructions:
Export async function phase7(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(7, 'Settle Task — Default Agent (No Split)').
2. Define taskId = 'CTO-1236', amount = 5_000_000 (5 USDC), qualityMet = true.
3. Compute receipt hash.
4. Derive PDAs — same as before but NO agent_package. The SettleTask struct has agent_package as Option<Account<AgentPackage>> and author_ata as Option<Account<TokenAccount>>. Pass null for both optional accounts.
5. Call settle_task WITHOUT agent_package and author_ata. In Anchor, optional accounts can be handled by passing null or not including them. Check how Anchor TypeScript client handles Option<Account>: typically you either omit the field or pass null. The approach may require passing the program ID itself as a placeholder for None accounts, or the Anchor client may accept null.
6. Fetch TaskReceipt — verify amount=5_000_000, author_earned=0, quality_met=true.
7. Log with chalk.yellow.bold: '💰 Full 5.00 USDC to treasury (no agent split)'.
8. Log: task_id, amount, treasury received full amount, Explorer link.

Note on Case 1 logic: No agent package means the full amount goes to treasury. The on-chain program should transfer the full amount from vault to treasury_ata.

description: Phase 8: Verify On-Chain Receipts — fetch and display formatted table
instructions:
Export async function phase8(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(8, 'Verify On-Chain Receipts').
2. Define the three task IDs: ['CTO-1234', 'CTO-1235', 'CTO-1236'].
3. For each task ID, derive the TaskReceipt PDA and fetch the account using state.program.account.taskReceipt.fetch(pda).
4. Build a formatted console table. Use string padding (padEnd/padStart) to align columns. Print a header row and separator:
```
  | Task ID   | Amount    | Author Earned | Quality      | Status  |
  |-----------|-----------|---------------|--------------|---------|  
```
5. For each receipt, format a row:
   - Task ID: receipt.taskId
   - Amount: formatUsdc(receipt.amount.toNumber()) + ' USDC'
   - Author Earned: formatUsdc(receipt.authorEarned.toNumber()) + ' USDC'
   - Quality: receipt.qualityMet ? '✅ MET' : '❌ NOT MET'
   - Status: map receipt.status enum to string (TaskStatus.Settled → 'Settled', etc.)
6. Print the table using console.log with chalk.white for data rows.
7. After the receipts table, fetch the AgentPackage PDA for 'smart-debug-agent-v1'.
8. Log agent package stats: total_earned (formatted USDC), task_count, success_count.
9. Expected values: total_earned = 3_000_000 (3 USDC from CTO-1234), task_count = 2 (CTO-1234 and CTO-1235 both used the package), success_count = 1 (only CTO-1234 had quality_met=true).

Note on Anchor enum deserialization: The TaskStatus enum in TypeScript will be represented as an object like { settled: {} } or a string. Check the Anchor IDL types file for the exact representation and handle accordingly when mapping to display strings.

description: Phase 9: Customer Withdraws Remaining Balance with final summary
instructions:
Export async function phase9(state: DemoState): Promise<void>.

Implementation:
1. Call phaseHeader(9, 'Customer Withdraws Remaining Balance').
2. Derive CustomerBalance PDA and fetch it to get the current balance.
3. The expected remaining balance is 185_000_000 (200 deposited - 10 for CTO-1234 - 5 for CTO-1236 = 185 USDC). CTO-1235 had quality_met=false so no charge.
4. Call state.program.methods.withdraw(new BN(remainingBalance)).accounts({ customer: state.customer.publicKey, customerBalance: customerBalancePda, customerAta: state.customerAta, vault: vaultPda, operatorConfig: operatorConfigPda, mint: state.mint, tokenProgram: TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID }).signers([state.customer]).rpc().
5. Fetch CustomerBalance again — verify balance is 0.
6. Log: withdrawal amount ('185.00 USDC'), final balance ('0.00 USDC'), Explorer link.
7. Print blank line, then final summary with chalk.bold.green:
   '🎉 Demo complete! Customer deposited 200 USDC, was charged 15 USDC for 2 successful tasks, was protected from 1 failed task, and withdrew 185 USDC.'
8. Print another line with chalk.dim showing the total demo execution time if available (passed from index.ts or computed locally).

description: Main entry point: CLI parsing, provider setup, sequential phase execution, error handling
instructions:
This is the main entry point that wires everything together.

Implementation:
1. Import parseArgs, getRpcUrl, createProvider, loadProgram from './cli.js'.
2. Import all phase functions from './phases/*.js'.
3. Import chalk for colored output and Connection/Keypair from @solana/web3.js.

4. Define the main async function:
   a. Parse CLI args.
   b. Log a banner with chalk.bold.magenta: '╔══════════════════════════════════════╗\n║   CTO Billing — End-to-End Demo      ║\n╚══════════════════════════════════════╝'
   c. Log cluster and RPC URL.
   d. Create Connection with getRpcUrl(cluster).
   e. Record start time with Date.now().
   f. Execute phase0 — get DemoState back.
   g. Set up the Anchor provider and program using the operator keypair from DemoState. Call createProvider(connection, state.operator) and loadProgram(provider). Attach program to state.
   h. Execute phases 1 through 9 sequentially, each receiving the state object.
   i. After phase 9, compute elapsed time: (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000.
   j. Log chalk.bold.green: 'All phases completed successfully in {elapsed}s'.

5. Wrap the entire main() call in a .catch handler:
   a. Log chalk.red.bold: 'Demo failed!'
   b. If the error is a SendTransactionError (from @solana/web3.js or Anchor), log the transaction logs: error.logs?.forEach(log => console.error(chalk.dim(log))).
   c. Log the error message and stack.
   d. Call process.exit(1).

6. Call main() at the bottom of the file.

IMPORTANT: Since this is an ESM module (type: module in package.json), all local imports must use .js extensions (e.g., './cli.js', './phases/phase0.js'). This is required for NodeNext module resolution with TypeScript.

title: CTO Billing Hackathon Demo Dashboard
content:
# CTO Billing — Hackathon Demo Dashboard

Next.js 14 web dashboard for the CTO Settlement Protocol on Solana devnet.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js >= 18
- A Solana wallet browser extension (Phantom or Solflare)
- The on-chain program deployed to devnet (see `programs/cto-billing/`)
- Program IDL at `target/idl/cto_billing.json` (generated by `anchor build`)

## Quick Start

```bash
cd demo/web
npm install
npm run dev          # http://localhost:3000
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_RPC_ENDPOINT` | `https://api.devnet.solana.com` | Solana JSON-RPC endpoint |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_PROGRAM_ID` | Value in `src/lib/constants.ts` | CTO Billing program ID |

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── app/
│   ├── layout.tsx              # Root layout with wallet providers
│   ├── page.tsx                # Split-panel main page
│   └── globals.css             # Tailwind + shadcn CSS variables
├── components/
│   ├── ui/                     # shadcn/ui components (button, card, badge, etc.)
│   ├── Header.tsx              # Logo, Devnet badge, wallet connect
│   └── WalletProviderWrapper.tsx  # Client-side wallet provider tree
├── hooks/
│   ├── index.ts                # Barrel export
│   ├── useProgram.ts           # Anchor Program instance hook
│   ├── usePdaDerivation.ts     # PDA derivation helpers (SHA-256 seeds)
│   ├── useCustomerBalance.ts   # Fetch CustomerBalance PDA data
│   └── useOperatorConfig.ts    # Fetch OperatorConfig PDA data
├── types/
│   └── index.ts                # On-chain type mirrors
├── lib/
│   ├── formatters.ts           # formatUsdc, formatQuality, shortenAddress
│   └── constants.ts            # PROGRAM_ID, seed prefixes
└── idl/
    └── cto_billing.json        # Anchor IDL (copied from target/)
```

## On-Chain Dependencies

This dashboard reads from and interacts with the following on-chain PDAs:

| Account | Seeds | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OperatorConfig` | `["operator_config"]` | Singleton: authority, treasury, mint, fee, paused |
| `CustomerBalance` | `["customer_balance", customer_pubkey]` | Per-wallet: balance, caps, counters |
| `AgentPackage` | `["agent_package", sha256(package_id)]` | Per-package: author, split, stats |
| `TaskReceipt` | `["task_receipt", sha256(task_id)]` | Per-task: immutable settlement record |
| `Vault` | `["vault", mint_pubkey]` | Token account: pooled customer deposits |

## Design System

- **Dark mode only** — Solana brand palette
- Primary: `#9945FF` (solana-purple)
- Success accent: `#14F195` (solana-green)
- Background: `#0E0E1A` (solana-dark)
- Card surface: `#1A1A2E` (solana-card)
- Component library: shadcn/ui (New York variant)
- Font: Inter via next/font/google
- Optimized for 1920×1080 screen recording

## Build & Verify

```bash
npm run build        # Production build — must complete with zero errors
npx tsc --noEmit     # Type check — must report zero errors
```

## Wallet Integration

Supported wallets: Phantom, Solflare. The app shows a "Not Connected" landing state with CTO branding when no wallet is connected. After connecting, the split-panel layout renders with a green glow indicator on the wallet button.

## Relationship to On-Chain Program

The dashboard imports the Anchor IDL generated by `anchor build` in `programs/cto-billing/`. The `useProgram()` hook constructs an Anchor `Program` instance using the connected wallet, enabling RPC reads of all PDA accounts. Task 8 (not in this setup task) will add live settlement feed and transaction submission UI on top of this foundation.

title: Dashboard Architecture & Data Flow
content:
# Dashboard Architecture

## Component Hierarchy

```
layout.tsx
└── WalletProviderWrapper (client boundary)
    ├── ConnectionProvider (endpoint from env)
    ├── WalletProvider (Phantom + Solflare adapters, autoConnect)
    └── WalletModalProvider
        └── page.tsx
            ├── Header
            │   ├── Logo (text-based CTO / 5D Labs)
            │   ├── NetworkBadge ("Devnet", solana-purple)
            │   └── WalletMultiButton (green glow when connected)
            └── MainContent
                ├── [not connected] → NotConnectedState
                └── [connected] → SplitPanelLayout
                    ├── LeftPanel (60%) — SettlementFeed placeholder
                    └── RightPanel (40%) — CustomerBalance placeholder
```

## Hook Data Flow

```
useConnection() ─────┐
                      ├──→ useProgram() → Program<CtoBilling> | null
useAnchorWallet() ────┘         │
                                ├──→ useCustomerBalance(wallet)
                                │      → program.account.customerBalance.fetchNullable(pda)
                                │      → { data, loading, error, refetch }
                                │
                                └──→ useOperatorConfig()
                                       → program.account.operatorConfig.fetchNullable(pda)
                                       → { data, loading, error, refetch }

usePdaDerivation()
  ├── deriveCustomerBalance(wallet)     seeds: ["customer_balance", wallet.toBuffer()]
  ├── deriveAgentPackage(packageId)     seeds: ["agent_package", sha256(packageId)]
  ├── deriveTaskReceipt(taskId)         seeds: ["task_receipt", sha256(taskId)]
  ├── deriveVault(mint)                 seeds: ["vault", mint.toBuffer()]
  └── deriveOperatorConfig()            seeds: ["operator_config"]
```

## PDA Seed Derivation — SHA-256 Alignment

The on-chain program (Task 1-2) uses `sha2::Sha256` to hash string identifiers into 32-byte seeds for PDA derivation. The dashboard **must** use the identical algorithm:

```typescript
// Must match: programs/cto-billing/src/utils/mod.rs → hash_seed()
import { sha256 } from 'js-sha256'; // or crypto.createHash('sha256')
const seed = Buffer.from(sha256(packageId), 'hex'); // 32 bytes
```

The `findProgramAddressSync` call is synchronous, so the SHA-256 implementation must also be synchronous.

## Wallet Provider Architecture Decisions

- **Client component boundary**: `WalletProviderWrapper.tsx` has the `'use client'` directive. The root `layout.tsx` remains a server component that delegates to this client wrapper.
- **Auto-connect**: Enabled via `autoConnect` prop so returning users reconnect automatically.
- **Adapter list**: Phantom and Solflare only (hackathon scope). The list is static and not dynamically detected.
- **Endpoint configuration**: Single RPC endpoint, configurable via `NEXT_PUBLIC_RPC_ENDPOINT`. No failover or load balancing for hackathon scope.

## Responsive Layout

| Viewport | Layout | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| ≥ 1024px | Side-by-side 60/40 | Optimized for 1920×1080 recording |
| < 1024px | Stacked vertically | Settlement feed on top, balance below |
| < 768px | Stacked, reduced padding | Header elements condensed |

## Integration Points with On-Chain Program

| Frontend Hook | On-Chain Account | Task Reference |
|---|---|---|
| `useOperatorConfig()` | `OperatorConfig` PDA | Task 1: `initialize_operator` |
| `useCustomerBalance()` | `CustomerBalance` PDA | Task 1: `create_customer_account`, `deposit`, `withdraw` |
| Future: Settlement feed | `TaskReceipt` PDA | Task 2: `settle_task`, `refund_task` |
| Future: Agent packages | `AgentPackage` PDA | Task 1: `register_agent_package` |

## Error Handling Strategy

- **Account not found**: `fetchNullable` returns null → display "No account" UI gracefully.
- **Wallet disconnected**: `useProgram()` returns null → show NotConnectedState.
- **RPC errors**: Caught in hooks, surfaced via `error` field. Toast notification for user-facing errors.
- **Build-time safety**: Strict TypeScript, all on-chain types mirrored as TS interfaces.

content:
# On-Chain Program Integration

## IDL Management

The Anchor IDL (`cto_billing.json`) is the contract between the on-chain program and the dashboard. It is generated by running `anchor build` in the project root.

**Workflow**:
1. `anchor build` generates `target/idl/cto_billing.json`
2. Copy to `demo/web/src/idl/cto_billing.json`
3. The `useProgram()` hook imports this IDL to construct the Anchor `Program` instance

If the on-chain program changes (new instructions, modified accounts), the IDL must be regenerated and re-copied.

## Program ID

Defined in `src/lib/constants.ts` and overridable via `NEXT_PUBLIC_PROGRAM_ID`. Must match `declare_id!()` in `programs/cto-billing/src/lib.rs` (Task 1).

## Account Reads (Current Scope — Task 7)

The dashboard currently performs **read-only** operations via Anchor's `program.account.<name>.fetchNullable(pda)` pattern:

| Hook | Account | Read Pattern |
|---|---|---|
| `useOperatorConfig()` | `OperatorConfig` | `program.account.operatorConfig.fetchNullable(pda)` |
| `useCustomerBalance(wallet)` | `CustomerBalance` | `program.account.customerBalance.fetchNullable(pda)` |

## Account Writes (Future — Task 8)

Task 8 will add transaction submission for:
- `deposit(amount)` — SPL token transfer from customer ATA to vault
- `withdraw(amount)` — SPL token transfer from vault to customer ATA
- `create_customer_account(maxPerTask, maxPerDay)` — First-time customer setup

These will use `program.methods.<instruction>(...args).accounts({...}).rpc()` with the connected wallet as signer.

## Settlement Pipeline Integration

The full settlement flow involves multiple system components:

```
[CodeRun completes] → [Controller billing hook (Task 4)]
    → Redis XADD "cto:settlements"
    → [Settlement Sidecar (Task 5)] XREADGROUP
    → Solana settle_task / refund_task transaction
    → [On-chain TaskReceipt PDA created (Task 2)]
    → [Dashboard reads TaskReceipt (Task 8)]
```

The dashboard sits at the end of this pipeline, reading the immutable `TaskReceipt` PDAs that the settlement sidecar creates on-chain.

## PDA Derivation Consistency

PDA seeds must be byte-identical across all components:

| Component | Language | SHA-256 Source |
|---|---|---|
| On-chain program (Task 1) | Rust | `sha2::Sha256::digest()` |
| Test suite (Task 3) | TypeScript | `crypto.createHash('sha256')` in `tests/helpers.ts` |
| Dashboard (Task 7) | TypeScript | `js-sha256` or `crypto.createHash('sha256')` in `usePdaDerivation()` |
| Settlement sidecar (Task 5) | Rust | `sha2::Sha256::digest()` in `crates/settlement-sidecar/src/pda.rs` |

All implementations hash the **UTF-8 encoded string** and produce a 32-byte digest used as the PDA seed.

## Devnet Testing

To test the dashboard against real on-chain data:
1. Deploy program to devnet: `anchor deploy --provider.cluster devnet`
2. Run initialization: execute `initialize_operator` and `create_customer_account` from tests (Task 3)
3. Fund customer wallet with devnet SOL and mock USDC
4. Start dashboard with `npm run dev` and connect the same wallet via Phantom

description: Settlement feed DataTable component displaying TaskReceipt records from on-chain program with polling, color-coded quality badges, and row selection

needs_context.reason: Need the TaskReceipt account struct and TaskStatus enum to correctly deserialize and display on-chain receipt data
Need PDA seed constants (TASK_RECEIPT_SEED) and hash_seed function to derive TaskReceipt PDAs client-side

needs_from_other_subtasks.description: Need the IDL TypeScript types (CtoBilling type), useProgram() hook, and useCustomerBalance() hook to access the Anchor program instance for fetching TaskReceipt accounts
Need formatUsdc() and formatTimestamp() utility functions, shortenAddress() helper, and the TaskReceipt/TaskStatus TypeScript type definitions from the shared types module
Need the Tailwind theme configuration (solana-card, solana-green, solana-purple color tokens) and shadcn component library setup to use DataTable, Badge, and other UI primitives

challenges: Fetching all TaskReceipt PDAs via program.account.taskReceipt.all() may be slow on devnet if there are many accounts — may need pagination or filters
Polling interval (5s) could cause UI flicker if the full account list is re-fetched each time; need to diff state to avoid unnecessary re-renders
The shadcn DataTable pattern requires defining columns with accessorKey/cell render functions which need careful typing against the Anchor IDL-generated types

description: Customer balance card component showing current balance, deposit/spent totals, task count, and spending cap progress bars with threshold-based color coding

needs_context.reason: Need the CustomerBalance account struct definition to understand all fields (balance, total_deposited, total_spent, task_count, max_per_task, max_per_day, daily_spent, daily_reset_day)

needs_from_other_subtasks.description: Need useCustomerBalance(publicKey) hook that fetches the CustomerBalance PDA and returns the deserialized account data, loading state, and refetch function
Need formatUsdc() utility for displaying USDC amounts and the CustomerBalance TypeScript type definition
Need Tailwind theme tokens (solana-card, solana-green) and shadcn Card, Progress, and Skeleton components set up

challenges: Computing the daily cap reset time from the on-chain daily_reset_day (which is likely a day number, not a timestamp) requires understanding the exact format used by the program's validate_daily_cap function
Progress bar color thresholds (70%/90%) need smooth CSS transitions that work well with Tailwind's dark theme utility classes

description: Deposit and Withdraw dialog components with USDC amount input, Anchor transaction submission, toast notifications, and automatic balance refresh on success

needs_context.reason: Need the Deposit accounts struct to know which accounts to pass when building the deposit transaction (customer, customer_balance, customer_ata, vault, operator_config, mint, token_program)
Need the Withdraw accounts struct to know which accounts to pass when building the withdraw transaction
Need VAULT_SEED, CUSTOMER_BALANCE_SEED, OPERATOR_CONFIG_SEED constants to derive PDAs client-side for transaction account resolution
Need BillingError variants to map program error codes to user-friendly messages in toast notifications

needs_from_other_subtasks.description: Need useProgram() hook for accessing the Anchor Program instance, and the PDA derivation utilities (deriveVaultPda, deriveCustomerBalancePda, deriveOperatorConfigPda) for building transaction accounts
Need formatUsdc() and any USDC lamport conversion utilities (e.g., parseUsdcToLamports) for converting user input amounts to on-chain u64 values
Need shadcn Dialog, Input, Button, and toast/Toaster components configured and available
Need the CustomerBalanceCard component's refetch callback or the useCustomerBalance hook's refetch function to trigger balance refresh after successful deposit/withdraw

challenges: Anchor's program.methods.deposit().accounts({...}).rpc() requires precisely matching account names and types from the IDL — any mismatch causes cryptic errors
Need to fetch the user's USDC ATA address and balance before submitting, using getAssociatedTokenAddress from @solana/spl-token, which adds an async dependency chain
Converting user-input decimal USDC (e.g., '10.50') to u64 lamports (10_500_000) requires careful handling of floating point precision — should use BN.js or integer math
The vault PDA is a token account that may need to be initialized separately if it doesn't exist yet — need to handle the first-deposit case

needs_context.reason: Need the AgentPackage account struct to understand all fields (package_id, author, split_bps, source_uri, total_earned, task_count, success_count, active)

Need formatUsdc(), shortenAddress(), and the AgentPackage TypeScript type definition
Need shadcn Card, Badge, Tabs, and Skeleton components, plus Tailwind theme tokens (solana-card, solana-purple)

Need OperatorConfig to understand protocol_fee_bps for computing the platform fee portion of the amount breakdown

Need formatUsdc(), formatTimestamp(), shortenAddress() utilities and TaskReceipt/TaskStatus TypeScript types
Need shadcn Card, Badge, Button, Dialog (if modal approach), and Tailwind theme tokens
Need PDA derivation functions (deriveReceiptPda) to construct the Solana Explorer URL from the task_id

Computing the platform fee requires knowing protocol_fee_bps from OperatorConfig, which means either fetching it separately or passing it through context — adds a data dependency

The SelectedReceiptContext needs to be created and wired into both the parent page layout and both consuming components (SettlementFeed and ReceiptDetail), requiring coordination with the page.tsx structure from Task 7
